hints.Nominative: While 'praesidium' could be nominative neuter, here it follows 'sub' + accusative (indicating motion toward)
hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'praesidium' would be 'praesidii' — 'praesidium' is accusative after 'sub'
hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'praesidium' would be 'praesidio' — here 'sub' takes accusative (motion toward)

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'praesidii' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'praesidii' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'praesidii' ends in -i

hints.to flee: 'to flee' is 'fugere' — 'confugimus' is from the compound 'confugere' (to flee for refuge)

hints.Perfect indicative: Perfect 1st pl. would also be 'confugimus' (same form), but context favors present tense
hints.Present subjunctive: Present subjunctive 1st pl. would be 'confugiamus' — 'confugimus' is present indicative
hints.Future indicative: Future 1st pl. would be 'confugiemus' — 'confugimus' is present tense

hints.fugio, fugere, fugi, fugitum: These are the principal parts of 'fugere' (to flee) — 'confugimus' is from the compound 'confugere'
hints.pugno, pugnare, pugnavi, pugnatum: These are the principal parts of 'pugnare' (to fight)
hints.confundo, confundere, confudi, confusum: These are the principal parts of 'confundere' (to confuse/pour together)

hints.Nominative: While the form is the same, 'Genetrix' is addressed directly — 'sancta Dei Genetrix' is vocative
hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'Genetrix' would be 'Genetricis' — 'Genetrix' is vocative here
hints.Accusative: Accusative of 'Genetrix' would be 'Genetricem' — 'Genetrix' is vocative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'Genetricis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'Genetricis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'Genetricis' ends in -is

hints.Nominative: Nominative is 'Deus' — 'Dei' has the -i genitive ending
hints.Dative: Dative of 'Deus' would be 'Deo' — 'Dei' ends in -i (genitive)
hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'Deus' would be 'Deo' — 'Dei' ends in -i (genitive)

translation: Under your protection we take refuge, holy Mother of God
